verses.text: Ahithophel urges Absalom to let him strike the weary David that very night, before he can recover. The plan is shrewd and swift, and it pleases everyone. The wisdom of the world, when it serves rebellion, can look unanswerable — and David's life hangs on a single night's decision.

Ahithophel urges Absalom to let him strike the weary David that very night, before he can recover. The plan is shrewd and swift, and it pleases everyone. The wisdom of the world, when it serves rebellion, can look unanswerable — and David's life hangs on a single night's decision.

Ahithophel urges Absalom to let him strike the weary David that very night, before he can recover. The plan is shrewd and swift, and it pleases everyone. The wisdom of the world, when it serves rebellion, can look unanswerable — and David's life hangs on a single night's decision.

Ahithophel urges Absalom to let him strike the weary David that very night, before he can recover. The plan is shrewd and swift, and it pleases everyone. The wisdom of the world, when it serves rebellion, can look unanswerable — and David's life hangs on a single night's decision.

Absalom calls also for Hushai, and Hushai dares to say Ahithophel's counsel is 'not good at this time.' One faithful voice rises against the consensus. God's deliverance often turns on someone willing to stand alone and speak against the crowd's confident plan.

Absalom calls also for Hushai, and Hushai dares to say Ahithophel's counsel is 'not good at this time.' One faithful voice rises against the consensus. God's deliverance often turns on someone willing to stand alone and speak against the crowd's confident plan.

Absalom calls also for Hushai, and Hushai dares to say Ahithophel's counsel is 'not good at this time.' One faithful voice rises against the consensus. God's deliverance often turns on someone willing to stand alone and speak against the crowd's confident plan.

Hushai paints David and his men as fierce as a bear robbed of her cubs, mighty veterans who will not be caught napping; one defeat will melt Israel's courage. He plays on Absalom's fear and vanity. Cunning meets cunning, and the LORD uses Hushai's words to stall the deadly haste.

Hushai paints David and his men as fierce as a bear robbed of her cubs, mighty veterans who will not be caught napping; one defeat will melt Israel's courage. He plays on Absalom's fear and vanity. Cunning meets cunning, and the LORD uses Hushai's words to stall the deadly haste.

Hushai paints David and his men as fierce as a bear robbed of her cubs, mighty veterans who will not be caught napping; one defeat will melt Israel's courage. He plays on Absalom's fear and vanity. Cunning meets cunning, and the LORD uses Hushai's words to stall the deadly haste.

Hushai proposes instead that all Israel gather, with Absalom himself leading the vast host — a grand, flattering picture that delays everything. The dramatic plan appeals to Absalom's pride. The slower, showier path he prefers is exactly the one that will cost him the war.

Hushai proposes instead that all Israel gather, with Absalom himself leading the vast host — a grand, flattering picture that delays everything. The dramatic plan appeals to Absalom's pride. The slower, showier path he prefers is exactly the one that will cost him the war.

Hushai proposes instead that all Israel gather, with Absalom himself leading the vast host — a grand, flattering picture that delays everything. The dramatic plan appeals to Absalom's pride. The slower, showier path he prefers is exactly the one that will cost him the war.

Absalom and all Israel judge Hushai's counsel better — 'for the LORD had appointed to defeat the good counsel of Ahithophel, to bring evil upon Absalom.' Here is the hidden hand. Men weigh advice and choose; God overrules the choice to accomplish His own purpose.

Hushai sends word to the priests: tell David not to linger but cross the Jordan tonight. The plan to save the king is set in motion. God's providence works through human messengers and urgent obedience; the warning must be carried, and carried quickly.

Hushai sends word to the priests: tell David not to linger but cross the Jordan tonight. The plan to save the king is set in motion. God's providence works through human messengers and urgent obedience; the warning must be carried, and carried quickly.

The priests' sons, the runners, are nearly caught, but a woman hides them in a well and covers it, throwing off the pursuers with a lie. Ordinary people risk much to shelter God's servants. The deliverance of the king passes through the courage of a few unnamed helpers.

The priests' sons, the runners, are nearly caught, but a woman hides them in a well and covers it, throwing off the pursuers with a lie. Ordinary people risk much to shelter God's servants. The deliverance of the king passes through the courage of a few unnamed helpers.

The message gets through, and David rises and brings all his people over the Jordan by morning light; not one is left behind. The danger of that night is past. When God grants escape, His people are to move at once and fully, leaving nothing to the pursuing enemy.

The message gets through, and David rises and brings all his people over the Jordan by morning light; not one is left behind. The danger of that night is past. When God grants escape, His people are to move at once and fully, leaving nothing to the pursuing enemy.

Ahithophel, seeing his counsel rejected, sets his house in order and hangs himself. The wisest head in Israel, robbed of influence, will not live with defeat. Pride that cannot bear to be overruled turns at last on itself; despair is the end of wisdom without God.

Absalom crosses Jordan with all Israel, with Amasa now over the army, and camps in Gilead facing David. The rebellion presses on, but it has lost the swift advantage and the counsel that might have won. What looks like strength has already begun, unseen, to fail.

Absalom crosses Jordan with all Israel, with Amasa now over the army, and camps in Gilead facing David. The rebellion presses on, but it has lost the swift advantage and the counsel that might have won. What looks like strength has already begun, unseen, to fail.

At Mahanaim, loyal men bring David beds, food, and supplies for his weary, hungry people. In exile he is not forsaken; God moves hearts to provide. When His servants are brought low, the LORD raises up friends to sustain them, and turns a flight into a place of provision.
